topical_clusters.description: Foundational protocols for reaching agreement in asynchronous systems with crash-stop or Byzantine failure models.

topical_clusters.theme: Eventual Consistency and Conflict-free Replicated Data Types (CRDTs)
topical_clusters.description: Mechanisms for optimistic replication and formal frameworks for ensuring convergence without global synchronization.
Scalable membership management and information propagation using probabilistic, peer-to-peer communication patterns.
Systems providing ACID properties across partitioned data stores, focusing on the trade-offs between latency and isolation levels.
Case studies and architectural post-mortems of production systems implementing the aforementioned theoretical concepts.

literature_matrix.title: In Search of an Understandable Consensus Algorithm
literature_matrix.approach: Raft consensus protocol (Leader election, Log replication, Safety).
literature_matrix.assumptions: Non-Byzantine, asynchronous network with eventual delivery.
literature_matrix.limitations: Performance bottleneck on a single leader; scale-out requires sharding.
literature_matrix.relevance: Primary comparison point for the thesis's proposed consensus optimization.

literature_matrix.title: Conflict-free Replicated Data Types
literature_matrix.assumptions: Monotonic semilattices for state merges; causal delivery for operations.
literature_matrix.limitations: High memory overhead for tombstone management in many implementations.
literature_matrix.relevance: Provides the formal basis for the thesis's edge-computing data synchronization.

literature_matrix.title: Highly Available Transactions: Virtues and Limitations
literature_matrix.approach: Formalization of 'Highly Available' (HA) transaction levels like Read Committed.
literature_matrix.assumptions: CAP theorem constraints; preference for Availability over strict Consistency.
literature_matrix.limitations: Proves that serializability cannot be achieved in HA settings.
literature_matrix.relevance: Defines the boundary of what my system can guarantee under partition.

literature_matrix.title: A Conflict-Free Replicated JSON Datatype
literature_matrix.approach: Algorithm for merging nested JSON structures without manual conflict resolution.
literature_matrix.assumptions: Collaborative editing environment; causal order preservation.
literature_matrix.limitations: Increased metadata overhead compared to flat CRDTs.
literature_matrix.relevance: Practical implementation guide for the thesis application layer.

literature_matrix.title: Spanner: Google’s Globally-Distributed Database
literature_matrix.approach: TrueTime API using GPS/Atomic clocks for external consistency.
literature_matrix.assumptions: Access to high-precision hardware clocks with bounded uncertainty.
literature_matrix.limitations: Hardware dependency makes it difficult to replicate in commodity clouds.
literature_matrix.relevance: Contrast to the thesis's software-only logical clock approach.

literature_matrix.title: Epidemic Algorithms for Replicated Database Maintenance
literature_matrix.approach: Anti-entropy and Rumor Mongering for database consistency.
literature_matrix.assumptions: Fixed set of replicas; periodic pairwise communication.
literature_matrix.limitations: Slow convergence for large state updates; high bandwidth usage if not tuned.
literature_matrix.relevance: Foundation for the background synchronization task in the thesis architecture.
